ground_truth:
**Multi-series line chart** showing ocean acidification trends from **1958 to 2018**.

**Title**: "Ocean acidification"

**Axes (verified 2026-06-19)**:
- X: "Year" — range **1958 to 2018**
- Left Y: "CO₂" — range **275 to 425** (ppm)
- Right Y: "pH" — range **8.03 to 8.33**

**Data series** (with discrete dots):
- **Atmospheric CO₂** (red): measured at Station Mauna Loa — 1958: ~315 ppm, 2018: ~415 ppm
- **Ocean pCO₂** (green): measured at Station ALOHA — starts 1990 at ~325 ppm, 2018: ~380 ppm
- **Ocean pH** (blue): measured at Station ALOHA — drops from **~8.12 to ~8.06** (taking rough averages; acidification trend)

**Inset map**: Hawaiian Islands with two stations marked (Mauna Loa as red triangle, ALOHA as red dot).

**Source context**: Data aligns with NOAA Mauna Loa Observatory (Keeling Curve) + Hawaii Ocean Time-series (HOT) program at Station ALOHA (running since 1988), but **no source citation is printed on the chart**.

---

responses.content:
# Chart Description

## (1) Chart type
Line chart with three data series.

## (2) Axes
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (ppm), range 275–425
- Right Y-axis: pH, range 8.03–8.33

## (3) Data series
- Red: Atmospheric CO₂ (measured at Station Mauna Loa)
- Green: Ocean pCO₂ (measured at Station Aloha)
- Blue: Ocean pH (measured at Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable values, outliers, inflection points
- Atmospheric CO₂: Starts ~315 ppm (1958), rises to ~415 ppm (2018)
- Ocean pH: Starts ~8.20 (1990s), declines to ~8.05 (2018)
- Ocean pCO₂: Starts ~350 ppm (1990s), rises to ~410 ppm (2018)
- Inflection point: ~1990s, where ocean pH begins steep decline after atmospheric CO₂ rise

## (5) Overall trend
Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ steadily increase. Ocean pH steadily decreases, indicating ocean acidification.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart type
Line chart (with multiple series).

## (2) Axes
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (ppm), range 275–425
- Right Y-axis: pH, range 8.03–8.33

## (3) Data series
- Red: Atmospheric CO₂ (measured at Station Mauna Loa)
- Green: Ocean pCO₂ (measured at Station Aloha)
- Blue: Ocean pH (measured at Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable values, outliers, inflection points
- Atmospheric CO₂: Starts ~315 ppm (1958), rises to ~415 ppm (2018)
- Ocean pH: Starts ~8.20 (1990s), declines to ~8.07 (2018)
- Ocean pCO₂: Starts ~350 ppm (1990s), rises to ~410 ppm (2018)
- Inflection point: ~1990s, where ocean pH begins steep decline coinciding with rising ocean pCO₂ and atmospheric CO₂.

## (5) Overall trend
Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ are increasing steadily. Ocean pH is decreasing steadily, indicating ocean acidification.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart type
Line chart with dual y-axes.

## (2) Axes with units and ranges
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (ppm), range 275–425
- Right Y-axis: pH, range 8.03–8.33

## (3) Data series with names
- Red line: Atmospheric CO₂ (measured at Station Mauna Loa)
- Green line: Ocean pCO₂ (measured at Station Aloha)
- Blue line: Ocean pH (measured at Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable values, outliers, and inflection points
- Atmospheric CO₂: Starts ~315 ppm (1958), rises to ~415 ppm (2018)
- Ocean pH: Starts ~8.20 (1958), declines to ~8.05 (2018)
- Ocean pCO₂: Starts ~350 ppm (1958), rises to ~415 ppm (2018)
- Inflection point: Around 1990, ocean pH begins accelerating downward; ocean pCO₂ begins accelerating upward.

## (5) Overall trend
Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ show consistent, steep increases over time. Ocean pH shows a consistent, steep decline, indicating ocean acidification. The decline in ocean pH is strongly correlated with the rise in atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart type
Line chart with multiple series.

## (2) Axes
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (ppm, range 275–425)
- Right Y-axis: pH (range 8.03–8.33)

## (3) Data series
- Red: Atmospheric CO₂ (measured at Station Mauna Loa)
- Green: Ocean pCO₂ (measured at Station Aloha)
- Blue: Ocean pH (measured at Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable values, outliers, inflection points
- Atmospheric CO₂: ~315 ppm (1958) → ~415 ppm (2018)
- Ocean pH: ~8.25 (1990s) → ~8.05 (2018)
- Inflection point: ~1990s, where ocean pH begins steep decline despite rising atmospheric CO₂
- Outliers: Ocean pH shows significant year-to-year variability (±0.05 units)

## (5) Overall trend
Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ show strong upward trends. Ocean pH shows a consistent downward trend, indicating ocean acidification.

# Chart Description

- Ocean pH: ~8.20 (early 1990s) → ~8.05 (2018)

Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ show consistent upward trends. Ocean pH shows a consistent downward trend, indicating ocean acidification.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart Type
Line chart with dual y-axes.

## (2) Axes
- **X-axis**: Year (1958–2018)
- **Left Y-axis**: CO₂ (ppm), range 275–425
- **Right Y-axis**: pH, range 8.03–8.33

## (3) Data Series
- Red: Atmospheric CO₂ (Station Mauna Loa)
- Green: Ocean pCO₂ (Station Aloha)
- Blue: Ocean pH (Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable Values & Trends
- Atmospheric CO₂: Started ~315 ppm in 1958, rose to >415 ppm by 2018.
- Ocean pH: Declined from ~8.22 to ~8.08 between 1994–2018.
- Ocean pCO₂: Increased from ~350 ppm to ~400 ppm between 1994–2018.
- No clear outliers; all series show consistent trends.

## (5) Overall Trend
All three variables show a clear upward trend in CO₂ and downward trend in pH, indicating ocean acidification.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart Type
Line chart with dual y-axes.

## (2) Axes with Units and Ranges
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (ppm) (275–425)
- Right Y-axis: pH (8.03–8.33)

## (3) Data Series with Names
- Red line: Atmospheric CO₂ (measured at Station Mauna Loa)
- Green line: Ocean pCO₂ (measured at Station Aloha)
- Blue line: Ocean pH (measured at Station Aloha)

## (4) Notable Values, Outliers, and Inflection Points
- Atmospheric CO₂: Starts ~315 ppm in 1958, rises steadily to ~415 ppm by 2018.
- Ocean pH: Starts ~8.2, declines steadily to ~8.0 by 2018.
- Inflection point: Around 1994, ocean pH begins its steeper decline.
- Outliers: No extreme outliers, but ocean pH shows high variability (error bars) from 1994 onward.

## (5) Overall Trend
Atmospheric CO₂ and ocean pCO₂ increase steadily over time. Ocean pH declines steadily, indicating ocean acidification. The decline in ocean pH correlates directly with the rise in atmospheric CO₂.

# Chart Description

## (1) Chart type
Line chart with two y-axes.

## (2) Axes with units and ranges
- X-axis: Year (1958–2018)
- Left Y-axis: CO₂ (parts per million, ppm) — 275 to 425
- Right Y-axis: pH — 8.03 to 8.33

## (3) Data series with names
- Red line: Atmospheric CO₂ measured at Station Mauna Loa
- Green line: Ocean pCO₂ measured at Station Aloha
- Blue line: Ocean pH measured at Station Aloha

## (4) Notable values, outliers, and inflection points
- Atmospheric CO₂: Started ~315 ppm in 1958, rose to ~415 ppm by 2018.
- Ocean pH: Started ~8.20, declined to ~8.06 by 2018 (steady decline with no major inflection points).
- Ocean pCO₂: Started ~350 ppm, rose to ~400 ppm by 2018.
- No major outliers; all series show consistent trends.

## (5) Overall trend
All three measurements show a clear upward trend in CO₂ and a downward trend in pH from 1958 to 2018, indicating ocean acidification.
